Any ideas on a character like Herbert?  After you pointed the base for Mason, Abhoth it felt strangely similar to Herbert. Specially the part about the filth creatures and abominations which he uses to do his bidding like Abhoth does.
 While Mason is on a psychological level, he's really making weird complex creatures in various shapes and sizes.
Abhoth description is used in 24 story to describe her
Herbert is based on Herbert from Re-Animator
guyz
in the last story chapter
where are all these genesis versions coming from?
why are they different from ramona? (in the sense that they seem to be separate entities, while gramona shares a body)
Salv's third text makes me think he will get a genesis version or at the very least a continuation of the temple light church storyline

in GRamona's side story, Doll introduces some sort of simulation machine that can bring awakers closer to their "origin" but I'm not sure about non-Mythag awakers or if Gelot is explained in her side story

Doll makes an experience to bring awakers closer to their origin
Ramon is the test subject ject
so Doll makes Ramona body acts as a gateway for Genesis
so GRamona and Ramona shares the same body since only GRamona mind enter this world
the true GRamona is still on her own world

Gelot is a different case since Herbert makes another vessel for her to come her
Gelot is not using Helot body
Seems like every new Genesis will come with their own physical body now so they are separated
imagine if we could summon our tulu and murphy to possess gtulu and gmurphy
This i know now, i read what you said. I just thought it was curious that Herbert's modus operandi is similar to the description of Abhoth. Not saying he's connected, but quite curious imo

Talking about Gversions. 24's special chapter gave the strange impression that her Gversion wouldn't be "24" but just "Mason". ||Each sona that becomes gold dust in the chapter, right after that, the base Mason we are with, becomes more inteligent (scholar), then wiser (joker), courageous (cat) and at end dangerous (shadow) when she admits having no remorse killing Albino||. The talk Joker had with Shadow gave the impression that if a personality's wish is fulfilled they become capable of returning to "Mason".
The chapter even ends with the protagonist saying one day they might get their memories back and Mason become whole again. It's quite the hopeful chapter imo

Herbert in Re-Animator believes he could overcomes death
This is similar to Morimens Herbert
since awakers can overcome death (they are literally walking corpses) so Herbert just experiements a lot
any bullshit he spew for his followers are probably not true

I had watched the Nosferatu movie and one sentence in the movies got me pondering about 24

Ohh true
Never paid much mind to it
The skilltree of her chapter, that has the interactions of 24 and Albino, he tried many treatments and some starts entering trances and having visions, with Salvador and i believe Wanda(?)
In the movie Nosferatu, the not!Van Helsing, tells the heroine something like "In another time you would've been a priestess of Isis"
And got me thinking
Maybe Mason was such a good experiment subject (according to Albino's opinion). Bc Mason is some kind of Shaman? Or has inclinations to the supernatural?
And then i read your post about her

blood moon, mirrors and a suffering priest
she probably saw Sal

lakes
ashes and lonely queen

pretty interesting
they really take the witch aspect in unexpected way XD
I like it
the only indication for that is her name Mason
Made me love her even more💖
Now we know her original self (Mason) was witch(y) too
from what she said this started after she being experimented on
Mason was just a normal worker try to make a living
Many types of shamans have visions after taking mind altering drugs. Yet in these cultures, being a shaman is something innate
That's why the Nosferatu sentence got me pondering. "in another time she could've been some sort of Shaman", since she wasn't just tripping, she was actualy having visions of people
